name: Portillo de La Sía y de los Tornos — por el nacimiento del Río Asón — circular desde Espinosa de los Monteros
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Espinosa De Los Monteros are found in the mountainous region of Las Merindades in northern Burgos, Spain. The area features rugged terrain with a blend of valleys and mountains, offering diverse challenges for road cyclists. Notable features include challenging mountain passes such as Picón Blanco, Portillo de la Sía, Lunada, and Estacas de Trueba, which contribute to the varied Espinosa De Los Monteros cycling terrain elevation difficulty. The landscape also includes oak groves and river valleys, providing varied scenery for rides. Best road cycling routes around Espinosa De Los Monteros The most popular road cycling route is Three Passes Loop: Portillo de la Lunada, El Caracol & Estacas de Trueba, a 57.7 miles (92.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain across multiple mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Portillo de La Sía y de los Tornos — por el nacimiento del Río Asón — circular desde Espinosa de los Monteros, a difficult 44.2 miles (71.1 km) path. This route navigates through mountain passes and near the source of the Asón River. Local road cyclists also love the Portillo de Lunada - Valle de Lunada Loop, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail leading through the Portillo de Lunada and Valle de Lunada, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Espinosa De Los Monteros is defined by challenging mountain passes, scenic valleys, and oak groves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Espinosa De Los Monteros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 1,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Espinosa De Los Monteros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Espinosa De Los Monteros, offering diverse challenges for all levels. The komoot community has created 29 tours in total, ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: The region features rugged, mountainous terrain with a blend of valleys and challenging mountain passes. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, scenic oak groves, and river valleys, providing varied scenery and demanding climbs.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: Yes, Espinosa De Los Monteros is renowned for its challenging mountain passes, making it a cyclist's paradise for experienced riders. Passes like Picón Blanco, Portillo de la Sía, Lunada, and Estacas de Trueba offer demanding ascents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057422954/three-passes-loop-portillo-de-la-lunada-el-caracol-estacas-de-trueba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Passes Loop: Portillo de la Lunada, El Caracol & Estacas de Trueba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with substantial elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057469207/portillo-de-la-sia-y-de-los-tornos-por-el-nacimiento-del-rio-ason-circular-desde-espinosa-de-los-monteros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de La Sía y de los Tornos — por el nacimiento del Río Asón — circular desde Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates through mountain passes and near the source of the Asón River.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in the area offer views of several significant natural features and landmarks. You can encounter challenging climbs to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Valnera Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón Blanco Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass through scenic areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lunada Valley&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sía Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estacas de Trueba Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 14 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the diverse and beautiful landscapes, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the region?
A: While the region is known for its challenging mountain passes, there are also options for easier rides. Out of the 29 available routes, 1 is classified as easy and 11 as moderate, providing choices for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling around Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: The mountainous nature of the region suggests that spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winters may bring snow to the higher passes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, the region offers several long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Matanela – Church of Vega de Pas loop from Quintana de los Prados&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38206921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Caracol – La Sía Pass loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt; is over 120 km, providing significant distances for endurance riders.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: Espinosa De Los Monteros serves as a common starting point for many routes, suggesting parking availability within or near the town. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the individual route details on komoot, as many routes begin directly from the town or accessible points in the surrounding area.

Q: Can I find viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Espinosa De Los Monteros?
A: Absolutely. Given the mountainous terrain and numerous passes, many routes offer spectacular viewpoints. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4347749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Posadía&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight known for its views, and the summits of passes like Picón Blanco and Castro Valnera also provide panoramic vistas.
tips: The lookout is near the road. and from there there are very beautiful landscapes. You can see a huge waterfall that flows into the Asón river. The photos are small with reality. It really is a wonder to watch.

A fantastic alternative to climb Los Tornos from Soba. A road in perfect condition where it is difficult to find another vehicle. The beginning of the itinerary to La Revilla is surrounded by forests and from Fresnedo the road changes slope and we can enjoy a panoramic view that goes from the Picón Blanco to the Sierra del Hornijo. In winter the view is even more spectacular with all those snowy mountains.
After 10 kilometers of ascent, without any notable difficulty, we reached the intersection with the Los Tornos road from where we only had 2.8 kilometers left to reach the pass.

Los Tornos via La Revilla and Fresnedo
